title: How to Rent a Boat Around Capri and the Bay of Naples
topic: How to rent a boat around Capri and the Bay of Naples
html:
The Bay of Naples is one of those rare corners of the Mediterranean where the scenery shifts every few minutes. One moment you are drifting past the pastel houses of Positano, the next you are nosing into a sea cave below the cliffs of Capri. Doing it by road means queues, hairpin bends and crowded ferries. Doing it by water means the whole coastline becomes your private route. The simplest way to start is to rent a boat for a day and let the captain or the charts decide where you wander.

Why the bay is made for a boat

This stretch of the Tyrrhenian coast packs an extraordinary amount into a short sail. From Naples and Sorrento you can reach Capri, Ischia and Procida in under an hour with the right vessel, and the Amalfi Coast unfolds to the south. Many of the best swimming spots, including the cliffs around the Faraglioni rocks and the quieter coves of Ischia, are simply easier to enjoy from the water than from a packed beach.

A boat also lets you sidestep the timetable that rules everyone else. Ferries fill quickly in summer, and the famous Blue Grotto can mean a long wait in a bobbing line of dinghies. With your own hire you arrive when the light is best and move on whenever you please.

Choosing the right type of vessel

The kind of boat you want depends on your group and your plans. A few common options work well here.

  • Small motorboats seat four to eight people and, depending on size and engine, may not require a licence. They are ideal for a relaxed day of swimming and short hops along the coast.
  • Gozzo boats, the classic wooden craft of the Sorrento peninsula, are built for comfortable cruising and shaded lunches at anchor.
  • RIBs are faster and better suited to reaching Capri or Ischia quickly when the sea is calm.
  • Sailing yachts and catamarans suit larger groups or anyone wanting an overnight trip, with room to relax and a steadier ride in open water.

If you would rather not handle the helm yourself, a skippered yacht charter includes a local captain who knows the currents, the hidden anchorages and the best time to slip into the grottoes.

With or without a skipper

Bareboat hire, where you take the wheel, can be wonderfully freeing if you hold the right qualifications and feel confident in busy summer traffic. The waters off Capri can get crowded, and local rules govern where you may anchor and swim, so experience counts.

For most visitors a skippered day is the easier choice. You gain a guide who speaks the coastline fluently, points out the spots the guidebooks miss, and handles the mooring while you swim. When you browse boats on a marketplace, the listing makes clear whether a skipper is included or available as an extra.

A sample day on the water

A typical hire runs from morning until early evening, which is plenty for a loop of the bay.

  1. Set off from Sorrento or Naples and cruise towards Capri while the sea is calm.
  2. Circle the island, pausing at the Green Grotto and the White Grotto for a swim in clear water.
  3. Anchor near the Faraglioni for lunch, either a picnic you brought or a stop at a waterside trattoria.
  4. Cross to Ischia or Procida in the afternoon for thermal coves and pastel harbours.
  5. Return as the cliffs catch the golden light, ideally with a glass of something cold in hand.
The best days on the bay are unhurried. Build in time to do nothing but float, because the view is the whole point.

Booking, costs and practical tips

Prices vary with the season, the size of the boat and whether fuel and a skipper are included. High summer between June and September is busiest, so reserve early to secure the better vessels. Spring and early autumn offer warm water, lighter crowds and gentler prices.

Before you go

  • Bring sun protection, water and soft-soled shoes that will not mark the deck.
  • Check the weather, as the bay can turn choppy when the wind rises in the afternoon.
  • Confirm what is included, from fuel and mooring fees to snorkelling gear and towels.
  • Carry cash for small harbour charges and tips for your skipper.

Comparing options in one place saves time. On BoatHire24 you can find a boat that matches your group, your budget and your itinerary, with photos, reviews and clear pricing. And if you own a vessel in the region and want it earning between trips, it takes only a few minutes to list your boat and reach travellers planning their own day around Capri.
